title: Destination Relationship Cleanup for Accommodation and EventLocation

tasks.title: Create AccommodationLocationSchema (postal address only)
tasks.description:
Create a new file `packages/schemas/src/entities/accommodation/accommodation.location.schema.ts` that defines a Zod schema for the postal address portion of an accommodation location — with no geographic fields (city, state, country, zipCode, neighborhood, department).

Files to create/modify:
- `packages/schemas/src/entities/accommodation/accommodation.location.schema.ts` (create)

Shape:
- coordinates: optional CoordinatesSchema
- street: optional string 2-50
- number: optional string 1-10
- floor: optional string 1-10
- apartment: optional string 1-10

Exports:
- `AccommodationLocationSchema` (Zod object)
- `AccommodationLocationType` (inferred TypeScript type)
- `AccommodationLocationFields` (spread helper for AccommodationSchema composition)

Test requirements:
- Unit test: parse a valid object — succeeds
- Unit test: parse object with city field — succeeds (passthrough) or stripped (strict), document the choice
- Unit test: export shape has no city/state/country keys

tasks.title: Create EventLocationAddressSchema (postal address only)
tasks.description:
Create a new schema that defines the postal address fields for eventLocations, removing city/state/country/neighborhood/department.

Files to create/modify:
- `packages/schemas/src/entities/event/event-location.address.schema.ts` (create, or inline in existing event-location.schema.ts)

Shape:
- coordinates: optional
- street: optional string (existing constraints)
- number: optional string
- floor: optional string
- apartment: optional string
- placeName: optional string (existing constraints)
- destinationId: UUID required FK

Exports:
- `EventLocationAddressSchema`
- `EventLocationAddressType`
- `EventLocationAddressFields`

Test requirements:
- Parse valid address — succeeds
- Parse address with city key — stripped or rejected consistently
- destinationId missing — fails with required error

tasks.title: Create CityDestinationRefSchema (relation projection)
tasks.description:
Create a lightweight destination reference schema used as the `cityDestination` field in API responses. This projection avoids sending heavy fields (media, reviewsCount, attractions) while giving the frontend enough data to render city name, breadcrumb, and SEO links.

Files to create/modify:
- `packages/schemas/src/entities/destination/destination.refs.schema.ts` (create, or add to existing destination.schema.ts if size permits)

Shape — pick from DestinationSchema:
- id (UUID)
- slug (string)
- name (string)
- summary (optional string)
- destinationType (DestinationTypeEnum)
- level (number)
- path (string)
- pathIds (array of UUIDs)

Exports:
- `CityDestinationRefSchema`
- `CityDestinationRef` (inferred type)

Test requirements:
- Parse a valid CITY destination subset — succeeds
- Parse an object missing `path` — fails
- Type assertion: `CityDestinationRef` has no `media` or `reviewsCount` keys

tasks.title: Replace BaseLocationFields with AccommodationLocationFields in AccommodationSchema
tasks.description:
Update `packages/schemas/src/entities/accommodation/accommodation.schema.ts` to import and spread `AccommodationLocationFields` (from T-001) instead of `BaseLocationFields`. Remove the city/state/country/zipCode mappings from the HTTP schema flatten/unflatten transforms.

Files to modify:
- `packages/schemas/src/entities/accommodation/accommodation.schema.ts`

- `packages/schemas/src/entities/accommodation/accommodation.crud.schema.ts` (if it references location fields)

Acceptance:
- `accommodation.location.city` is a TypeScript error after change
- `accommodation.destinationId` remains required
- HTTP schema flat→nested mapping no longer includes city/state/country

Test requirements:
- Existing accommodation schema tests updated: fixtures drop city/state/country from location block
- TypeScript typecheck passes with no `city` on `AccommodationLocationType`

tasks.title: Restructure EventLocationSchema to use new address fields
tasks.description:
Rewrite `EventLocationSchema` in `packages/schemas/src/entities/event/event-location.schema.ts` to:
- Remove city, state, country, zipCode, neighborhood, department fields
- Add `destinationId` (UUID, required)
- Keep id, slug, placeName, street, number, floor, apartment, coordinates, audit, lifecycle, admin fields

- Downstream event schemas that extend or reference EventLocationSchema: EventSchema, EventPublicSchema, EventAdminSchema (audit for override references to removed fields)

Acceptance:

- `eventLocation.destinationId` is required string (UUID)

Test requirements:

- Add test: EventLocationSchema with destinationId missing fails

tasks.title: Revert BBT-11 hotfix (city field on BaseLocationSchema)
tasks.description:
Remove the `city` field added in commit `7d19f59c` from `BaseLocationSchema`. That additive hotfix is now superseded by the structural fix in this spec — accommodation and eventLocation no longer inherit from BaseLocation for their geographic context.

Files to modify:
- `packages/schemas/src/common/location.schema.ts` — remove the `city` optional field block
- `.claude/docs/BEFORE_BETA_TESTING.md` or equivalent BBT document — update item 11 to note that the structural fix in SPEC-095 supersedes this hotfix

Acceptance:
- `BaseLocationSchema` shape is exactly: state, zipCode, country, coordinates (pre-hotfix shape)
- `city` does not appear on BaseLocationSchema or BaseLocationType
- Any remaining callers of BaseLocationSchema (user.location, destination.location) are NOT broken by this revert — they do not need city

Test requirements:
- BaseLocationSchema unit test: city key not in schema shape
- Run grep `BaseLocationSchema` to audit all consumers compile cleanly

tasks.title: Update DB column types for accommodation and event_location JSONB
tasks.description:
Update the Drizzle schema files to reflect the new postal-address-only types in the JSONB location column, and add `destinationId` to event_locations.

Files to modify:
- `packages/db/src/schemas/accommodations.dbschema.ts` — change `$type<FullLocationType>()` to `$type<AccommodationLocationType>()`
- `packages/db/src/schemas/event_locations.dbschema.ts` — change location JSONB type to `$type<EventLocationAddressType>()`, add `destinationId: uuid().references(() => destinations.id).notNull()`

After schema change:
1. Run `pnpm db:fresh-dev` (push-only, no numbered migration needed per project policy)
2. Run `packages/db/scripts/apply-postgres-extras.sh` to re-apply triggers/views/CHECKs

Acceptance:
- `drizzle-kit push` completes without error
- `apply-postgres-extras.sh` completes without error
- DB column introspection shows event_locations.destination_id FK to destinations

Test requirements:
- Introspection/schema test: verify event_locations has destinationId column (if applicable per project testing standard for push-only)
- No data migration needed (no production data)

tasks.title: Add CITY destinationType validation to AccommodationService (create + update)
tasks.description:
Implement the `destinationType === CITY` guard in `AccommodationService._beforeCreate` and `AccommodationService._beforeUpdate`. Load the destination from `DestinationModel` (or `DestinationService`) and reject with `ServiceError(VALIDATION_ERROR)` if the type is not CITY.

Files to modify:
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation.service.ts` (or equivalent path)

Acceptance:
- `_beforeCreate` with PROVINCE destinationId → throws VALIDATION_ERROR with message 'destinationId must reference a destination of type CITY'
- `_beforeCreate` with CITY destinationId → no error, create proceeds
- `_beforeUpdate` with non-CITY destinationId → same rejection
- destinationId pointing to non-existent destination → NOT_FOUND (existing behavior preserved)

Test requirements (co-located with implementation):
- Test: create with PROVINCE dest → VALIDATION_ERROR
- Test: create with CITY dest → success
- Test: update changing to PROVINCE dest → VALIDATION_ERROR
- Use mocked DestinationModel to avoid DB in unit test

tasks.title: Add CITY destinationType validation to EventLocationService (create + update)
tasks.description:
Implement the `destinationType === CITY` guard in `EventLocationService._beforeCreate` and `EventLocationService._beforeUpdate`. The destination relationship for events lives entirely on the eventLocation entity — EventService only holds a `locationId` FK and does NOT carry destinationId directly.

Files to modify:
- `packages/service-core/src/services/eventLocation/eventLocation.service.ts` — add guard in `_beforeCreate` and `_beforeUpdate`

Load the destination from `DestinationModel` (or `DestinationService`) and reject with `ServiceError(VALIDATION_ERROR)` if the type is not CITY.

Acceptance:
- `_beforeCreate` with PROVINCE destinationId → VALIDATION_ERROR 'destinationId must reference a destination of type CITY'
- `_beforeCreate` with CITY destinationId → no error, create proceeds
- `_beforeUpdate` with non-CITY destinationId → same rejection
- Non-existent destinationId → NOT_FOUND (existing behavior preserved)

Test requirements:
- Test: create with PROVINCE dest → VALIDATION_ERROR
- Test: create with CITY dest → success
- Test: update to PROVINCE dest → VALIDATION_ERROR
- Use mocked DestinationModel to avoid DB in unit test

tasks.title: Eager-load cityDestination in AccommodationService list and search

- Response transformer that maps model rows to public/admin shape — add `cityDestination` field by parsing with `CityDestinationRefSchema`

Acceptance:
- `list` and `search` responses include `cityDestination` on each item
- `cityDestination` fields match CityDestinationRefSchema shape (id, slug, name, summary, destinationType, level, path, pathIds)
- No extra destination fields (media, reviewsCount) in the response

Test requirements:

- Test: cityDestination contains only the 8 permitted fields

tasks.title: Eager-load cityDestination in EventLocationService response methods
tasks.description:
Add destination eager-loading and `cityDestination` projection to the EventLocationService response methods. The destination is a relation of eventLocation, NOT of EventService. EventService exposes events with an embedded eventLocation — the eager-load must happen on the eventLocation side so that every route returning an event with its eventLocation already has `cityDestination` populated.

Files to modify:
- `packages/service-core/src/services/eventLocation/eventLocation.service.ts` — update `getDefaultListRelations` and `getDefaultGetByIdRelations` (or equivalents) to include the destination relation
- Response transformer for eventLocation shape — add `cityDestination` field projected via `CityDestinationRefSchema`
- Verify: any route that returns an event with eventLocation embedded (e.g. EventService.getById with relations) must be confirmed to receive the already-loaded cityDestination from eventLocation — no additional join needed at the EventService level if the relation is correctly loaded on eventLocation

Acceptance:
- EventLocationService list/getById responses include `cityDestination`
- All event API responses that embed eventLocation include `cityDestination`
- Shape matches CityDestinationRefSchema (id, slug, name, summary, destinationType, level, path, pathIds)
- No extra destination fields (media, reviewsCount) in the response

Test requirements:
- Test: eventLocation list response items have `cityDestination.name`
- Test: eventLocation getById response has `cityDestination`
- Test: event getById response (with embedded eventLocation) has `eventLocation.cityDestination`

tasks.title: Update transforms.ts to derive cityName, cityPath, cityDestinationSlug from cityDestination
tasks.description:
Update `apps/web/src/lib/api/transforms.ts` — and any sibling transform files for events — to read from `cityDestination` instead of `location.city`.

Files to modify:
- `apps/web/src/lib/api/transforms.ts` — `transformAccommodation` and `transformEvent` (or equivalents)

For each transform, derive:
- `cityName: string` from `cityDestination.name`
- `cityPath: string` from `cityDestination.path` (for SEO link to /destinos)
- `cityDestinationSlug: string` from `cityDestination.slug`

Defensive fallback: if `cityDestination` is null/undefined (should not happen post-migration), return empty strings and log a warning via `@repo/logger`.

All derived values are computed once in the transform — components receive flat string props.

Acceptance:
- `location.city` is never read in the transform function bodies
- `transformAccommodation` returns an object with `cityName`, `cityPath`, `cityDestinationSlug`
- Same for `transformEvent`

Test requirements:
- Unit test: transform with valid cityDestination → correct cityName/cityPath/cityDestinationSlug
- Unit test: transform with null cityDestination → empty strings + warning log

tasks.title: Update LodgingBusinessJsonLd to build address from cityDestination data

Derivation logic:
- `addressLocality` = `cityName` prop

- `addressCountry` = `AR` for MVP (hardcoded, documented in comment as acceptable until multi-country support needed)

- Zero reads of `location.city` in the component

tasks.title: Create CityDestinationPicker island component
tasks.description:
Create a new React island `apps/web/src/components/form/CityDestinationPicker.client.tsx` that provides an autocomplete UX for selecting a CITY destination.

Behavior:
- On focus or keypress, query `GET /api/v1/public/destinations?type=CITY&search={input}` (debounced, min 2 chars)
- Display matching results in a dropdown list (name + path breadcrumb)
- On selection, update form state: set `destinationId` to selected id, store display name locally
- Show a 'No encuentro mi ciudad' link below the input at all times (or when no results)
- Clicking the link opens the feedback/contact page prefilled with subject='Solicitud de nueva ciudad'

Files to create:
- `apps/web/src/components/form/CityDestinationPicker.client.tsx`

Acceptance:
- Component queries with type=CITY filter
- Selection updates destinationId in parent form state
- 'No encuentro mi ciudad' link is always visible
- Keyboard navigation works (arrow keys, enter, escape)

Test requirements:
- Unit test: typing triggers debounced fetch
- Unit test: selection calls onSelect(id, name)
- Unit test: 'No encuentro mi ciudad' link is rendered
